blocks.content: Things with Ryan were still going. Kit had stopped counting the weeks. He'd made a kind of peace with it — not acceptance exactly, more like the particular numbness that set in when something hurt long enough. He was functional. He showed up at school. He was good at the things he was good at. He and Daisy still walked to school some mornings and talked on AIM and sat in the same place at lunch, and if there was a version of himself that ached every time Ryan Callaway appeared, that version had learned to stay quiet.
On the last Friday of the year, after school, Daisy found him at his locker.

They got their bikes and rode in the easy way they'd been riding since they were eight — Daisy taking the corner wide, Kit on the inside — and for the length of the ride it was just them, the year and the warm afternoon and the smell of spring going into summer.
She didn't answer. They rode on, and the afternoon was warm, and Kit thought: sophomore year. He'd have a whole year to figure it out. He had time.
He told himself he had time.
